🔴 DOCTOR OR TERRORIST? The IDF eliminated a Hamas terrorist who operated as a hospital doctor, Jamal Abu Aoun.

He served as a company commander in Hamas’ military wing, advancing and carrying out attack plans, while simultaneously working as a doctor at Al-Yafa Hospital in Deir al-Balah.

Recently, in violation of the ceasefire agreement, Abu Aoun advanced Hamas’ efforts to rehabilitate and rebuild.

R to @Mdais: Summary of the attack at the Gush Etzion Junction:

MDA EMTs and paramedics provided medical treatment and evacuated two casualties to Shaare Tzedek:
A 17-year-old girl in serious condition with lower limb injuries, and a 15-year-old girl in mild condition with a facial injury.

Update on the terrorist attack at the Gush Etzion Junction:

MDA EMTs and paramedics are treating a 17-year-old girl in serious condition with injuries to her limbs, and a 15-year-old girl in moderate condition with a facial injury, and evacuating them to Shaare Tzedek Hospital.

📸‼️These are photos from the precise IDF operation in the Beaufort Ridge and Wadi Saluki area in southern Lebanon.

It’s our pleasure to serve you.

⭕️ The IDF launched an operation, beginning a few days ago, in the Beaufort Ridge and Wadi al-Saluki area in southern Lebanon in order to dismantle terrorist infrastructure, eliminate terrorists and remove direct threats to Israeli civilians.

The operation is focused on establishing operational control of the Beaufort Ridge and the Wadi al-Saluki area, as well as degrading Hezbollah & dismantling terrorist infrastructure that was established on the ridge under Iranian direction. 

From Beaufort Ridge, Hezbollah terrorists managed military & combat activities and carried out numerous attacks.

The IDF has crossed the Litani River and expanded its operations against Hezbollah targets north of the river. The operation is currently expanding to additional areas.

R to @mofauae: In light of the evolving public health situation related to Ebola virus disease (EVD), the Ministry of Foreign Affairs advises UAE nationals and residents against travel to the Republic of Uganda, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and the Republic of South Sudan unless absolutely necessary.
UAE nationals currently present in these countries are urged to exercise the utmost caution, follow health and safety instructions issued by the local authorities, register in the Twajudi service, and contact the Ministry in case of emergencies.
#UAEMoFA
